title: Fusha parser/checker grammar issue
description: A first-class, source-addressed, routable grammar diagnostic emitted by the Fusha parser/checker. It records WHAT is wrong (issue_class), WHERE (target_address + the exact token/card graph edge), HOW BAD (severity + GP0 gate), WHERE TO SEND IT (route_to), and a source-clean learner-facing explanation. Internal evidence labels never reach a public surface. See parserplans/008-grammar-issue-taxonomy.md.

issue_id.description: Stable, namespaced, never-recycled id (the regression-test anchor), e.g. fusha.nahw.host_only_preposition.

target_address.description: REQUIRED. The exact source address the issue is about. An issue without a source address is invalid (validator FAIL condition 1).

token_or_card_edge.description: REQUIRED. The exact graph edge the issue attaches to: a token loc (quran:S:A:W / wbw:S:A:W), an example-card usage address (qamus:<id>#usage=S:A), or a segment ref (loc:G). Validator FAIL condition 1.

trigger_address.description: The governor/cause token, recorded SEPARATELY from the error token (e.g. the particle that forces naṣb). Lets the explanation point at the cause, not just the symptom.

codes.description: Optional multi-tag codes (an error can be morphological AND syntactic at once).

gate.description: Canonical GP0 4-tier gate. A two-vote/iʿrāb-sensitive issue marked auto_safe is invalid (validator FAIL condition 3).

route_to.description: REQUIRED. Where the diagnostic is sent. An issue that cannot route is invalid (validator FAIL condition 6).

procedure.description: A repo-relative path to the resolving procedure/drill/report (must exist on disk).

explanation.description: Source-clean, learner-facing 'because' explanation. Teaches the Arabic; never leaks source names/process prose (validator FAIL condition 2).

observed.description: The observed feature/value in the claim.
